label: Repo Workflow Ready Packet Starter
summary: Starter contract for a maintainer-facing skill-shaped slice that reads repo-local workflow truth, pairs it with the current GitHub-connected summary, and prepares a non-mutating readiness packet.

starterPack.audience: maintainers and builders drafting future skill-shaped integrations
starterPack.role: official repo-owned skill product line surface
starterPack.packageShape: machine-readable manifest + install/use note + bounded contract files
starterPack.notFor: claiming a shipped formal Skills runtime, marketplace listing, SDK, or hosted API

openApiBridge.note: Compatibility bridge only. This does not claim a hosted API product.

repoLocalPacket.note: Maintainer-facing only. This packet reshapes truth for review readiness but does not mutate GitHub.

inputs.name: workspaceRoot
inputs.description: Workspace root for the repo-local workflow summary or readiness packet.

inputs.name: failedRunsLimit
inputs.description: Optional cap for recent failed GitHub workflow runs when building the readiness packet.

outputs.name: workflowSummary
outputs.description: Read-only repo-local plus GitHub-connected workflow snapshot.

outputs.name: workflowReadyPacket
outputs.description: Maintainer-facing readiness packet with repo-local, GitHub-connected, remote-mutation, and external-blocker separation.

limitations: Local stdio MCP is still the primary builder surface.
The OpenAPI mention is a compatibility bridge, not a hosted API promise.
The repo-local packet is maintainer-facing and intentionally non-mutating.
This example does not prove a formal Skills product, plugin distribution, SDK packaging, or write-capable remote MCP surface.

proofAnchors.why: Freezes the builder-surface order and names the current repo-owned skill product line via @openui/skills-kit.
Locks MCP primary, OpenAPI compatibility secondary, and repo-local workflow tertiary.
Repeats the current builder-facing order while keeping the repo-owned skill product line bounded.
Defines the runtime entrypoint and builder-facing order.
Shows the maintainer-facing readiness packet is non-mutating and artifact-producing.
Confirms the MCP server is the protocol entrypoint.
Defines the skill product line audience, role, install path, and boundaries.
Provides the shortest public install/use route for zero-context maintainers.

nextActivationBoundary: If this starter slice becomes a public builder surface, update README and the builder-surface truth ledger in the same change.
If this slice gains formal runtime registration, add contract-aware verification for the new invocation path.
If this slice becomes more than maintainer-facing packaging, decide whether it belongs to a future SDK, plugin, or hosted API lane before changing the wording.
